title: Consolidated Bibliography
description: Every citation_id used anywhere in this repository resolves here. Entries carry a verification_status that MUST be read before use.

CRITICAL_WARNING_READ_BEFORE_CITING.statement: Most entries below carry verification_status: cited_from_search_result. That means the bibliographic details came from a search engine's summary and NOBODY HAS OPENED THE SOURCE.
what_could_be_wrong: Author lists may be incomplete or wrong.
Years may be the publication year, the online-first year, or the accessed year — these differ and reviewers notice.
Volume, issue, and page numbers are frequently absent or wrong.
The finding attributed to the source may be a paraphrase of a paraphrase.
Some URLs may have rotted since indexing.
A few entries below are placeholders for well-known works whose exact details have NOT been checked at all — they are marked needs_verification.
CRITICAL_WARNING_READ_BEFORE_CITING.the_rule: Open every source before it appears in a submitted thesis or an investor document. A reference list you have not read is an academic integrity problem and a diligence liability. This is not pedantry: a committee member who finds one fabricated citation will disbelieve your entire bibliography, and they will be right to.
CRITICAL_WARNING_READ_BEFORE_CITING.how_to_verify: Retrieve the DOI, confirm the authors and year against the publisher's page, confirm the finding is actually in the text, and update verification_status to url_checked with an accessed date.

citation_style: APA 7th edition. Pre-rendered strings in the `apa7` field are BEST-EFFORT and inherit the verification problem above.

authors: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations
references.title: The State of World Fisheries and Aquaculture 2024: Blue Transformation in action
references.apa7: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations. (2024). The state of world fisheries and aquaculture 2024: Blue transformation in action. FAO. https://doi.org/10.4060/cd0683en
references.relevance: THE foundational statistic source for this entire project. Establishes that aquaculture surpassed capture fisheries in aquatic animal production for the first time in 2022 (94.4 Mt, 51 percent), that inland systems are 62.6 percent of farmed aquatic animals, and that Asia produces 70 percent of global output. Every market argument this company makes traces here.
key_figures_used: 94.4 Mt aquaculture aquatic animals, 51 percent share
references.verification_note: The DOI and figures come from FAO's own online summary pages and are likely correct, but SOFIA 2026 may now supersede this edition. Check for a newer edition before submission — citing a superseded flagship report signals carelessness.

references.title: Reducing disease risk in aquaculture
references.container: World Bank, Agriculture and Environmental Services Discussion Paper 09, Report No. 88257-GLB
references.apa7: World Bank. (2014). Reducing disease risk in aquaculture (Agriculture and Environmental Services Discussion Paper No. 09, Report No. 88257-GLB). World Bank.
references.relevance: Source for the FAO estimate of ~USD 6 billion in annual global aquaculture disease losses, and for the EMS/AHPND Mekong Delta figure of ~USD 800 million per year borne mostly by small-scale producers. Used to size the disease problem class — with the explicit caveat that the OmniDrone addresses almost none of it.

references.title: Asian shrimp production and the economic costs of disease
references.apa7: Shinn, A. P., Pratoomyot, J., Griffiths, D., Trong, T. Q., Vu, N. T., Jiravanichpaisal, P., & Briggs, M. (2018). Asian shrimp production and the economic costs of disease. Asian Fisheries Science, 31S, 29-58.
references.relevance: Source for WSSV loss figures — USD 6 billion during the 1992-93 Asian outbreaks, USD 1-2 billion annually thereafter. Sizes the disease problem class.
references.verification_note: Author list and year are RECONSTRUCTED from partial search results and are NOT confirmed. Do not cite until the actual paper is retrieved. This is exactly the kind of entry that gets a thesis marked down.

references.title: Incidence and causes of mass fish kill in a shallow tropical eutrophic lake (Laguna de Bay, Philippines)
references.relevance: THE MOST SCIENTIFICALLY IMPORTANT PHILIPPINE SOURCE IN THIS BIBLIOGRAPHY. Identifies the causal triad behind mass kills — dissolved oxygen depletion, high ammonia-nitrogen, and hydrogen sulfide — which is the signature of a post-overturn event delivering bottom water to the surface. Three of the probe's sensor channels exist because of this finding. This is the citation the thesis should lead with when justifying the vertical profiling architecture.
references.verification_note: Authors and year are UNKNOWN. Retrieve from the SEAFDEC repository and complete this entry properly. Given how load-bearing this citation is for the entire technical argument, leaving it incomplete is not acceptable — this should be the first source verified.

references.title: Occurrence and recurrence: the fish kill story in Lake Buhi, Philippines
references.relevance: Framing source for the argument that Philippine fish kills are a RECURRING PATTERN rather than a series of accidents — which is the distinction between bad luck and an unmet monitoring need. Also the source for the estimated PHP 322.1 million in recorded national fish kill losses 1998-2018.
references.verification_note: Author list not captured. The PHP 322.1 M figure needs checking against the paper's actual methodology — see the contradiction documented in cost-of-inaction.json, where this national figure appears irreconcilable with individual event figures.

references.title: Fish kill hits Taal Lake anew; 343 metric tons of tilapia, 'bangus' turn belly up
references.relevance: Source for the 343-tonne Taal Lake event and its municipal breakdown (Talisay 300 t, Agoncillo 32 t, Laurel 11 t). The spatial concentration — 87 percent of the loss in one municipality — is direct evidence for the spatial-coverage argument against single-point monitoring.
references.verification_note: News reporting of fish kill tonnages is frequently revised in later reports. Confirm this figure was not superseded. Journalism is acceptable for establishing that an event occurred; it is weak evidence for a precise quantity in a thesis.
references.source_quality_note: Journalistic. Adequate for a pitch, weak for a dissertation. Seek BFAR's primary incident records.

references.title: BFAR assures ample supply of tilapia amid fish kill
references.relevance: Source for the 600+ tonne / PHP 43.13 million tilapia kill figure and for the attribution to over-stocking driving DO depletion. Also the source for BFAR's stated normal surface DO of 6 ppm at Taal — a useful local operational threshold.
references.verification_note: Date not captured. The 600 t and PHP 43.13 M figures must be confirmed to refer to the same event and scope before the derived PHP/tonne figure in incident-case-studies.json is used anywhere.

references.title: Massive fish kill hits Pangasinan town
references.relevance: Source for the ~PHP 15 million bangus loss in Pangasinan, attributed to extreme heat and low rainfall. Evidence for the climate-trigger argument.

references.title: Extreme heat, low rainfall trigger massive fish kill in northern Philippines
references.relevance: Corroborating source for heat-driven mortality. The mechanism matters: heat kills through a pincer — rising temperature increases fish metabolic oxygen demand while decreasing oxygen solubility. Both sides are directly measurable by the probe.
references.source_quality_note: Foreign general-interest outlet reporting on a Philippine event. Weak. Find the primary BFAR report.

references.title: Environmental impacts of aquaculture in the Philippines
references.relevance: Supports the reflexive-degradation argument: aquaculture effluent drives the eutrophication of the shared water body that then harms aquaculture. Directly relevant to the commons-problem framing in problem-taxonomy.json.

authors: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations
references.title: Nutrient dynamics in eutrophic inland waters used for aquaculture (Philippines, Thailand, Hong Kong)
references.relevance: Supports the eutrophication and self-degradation argument in cost-of-inaction.json — the feedback loop where uneaten feed degrades the water that then causes the losses.

references.title: The role of dissolved oxygen in aquaculture systems
references.relevance: General reference on DO's role.
references.source_quality_note: VENDOR CONTENT MARKETING. Not a peer-reviewed source. Acceptable as a pointer to established practice; NOT acceptable as an authority in a thesis. Replace with Boyd or a comparable textbook before submission.

references.title: Water quality: An introduction
references.relevance: The standard reference for aquaculture water quality. Cited throughout for DO thresholds, diel cycles, supersaturation interpretation, and general water chemistry. This is the authority that should replace the vendor content marketing above.
references.verification_note: Edition, year, and DOI NOT CONFIRMED. Claude Boyd is unambiguously the standard author in this field and this book exists, but the specific edition details here are from memory and MUST be checked against a library catalogue before citing. Do not submit this entry as written.
references.priority: HIGH — this is a load-bearing general citation and it needs to be correct

references.title: Normalized difference chlorophyll index: A novel model for remote estimation of chlorophyll-a concentration in turbid productive waters
references.relevance: The originating paper for NDCI, which is the core index in the OmniDrone's imaging pipeline. Critical because it addresses turbid productive waters specifically — the regime where classic blue-green ocean chlorophyll algorithms fail, and exactly the regime of a eutrophic tropical fish pond.
references.verification_note: Details reconstructed from general knowledge. Volume, pages, and DOI MUST be confirmed. This is a foundational technical citation for the imaging claim and it needs to be right.

references.title: The use of the Normalized Difference Water Index (NDWI) in the delineation of open water features
references.relevance: The originating NDWI paper. Water masking is the first step in the imagery pipeline — nothing downstream is valid without it.

references.title: Assessing drone-based remote sensing for monitoring water temperature, suspended solids and CDOM in inland waters: A global systematic review of challenges and opportunities
references.relevance: A 2024 systematic review of exactly our problem domain. Critical for understanding the documented challenges — radiometric calibration, glint, and the ground-truthing burden — that the OmniDrone's carry-your-own-calibration architecture is designed to address. READ THIS BEFORE BUILDING ANYTHING.
references.verification_note: DOI captured and likely correct. Author list not captured.
references.priority: HIGH — most directly relevant methodological review

references.title: Drone-based digital twins for water quality monitoring: A systematic review
references.relevance: THE CLOSEST PRIOR ART TO THIS EXACT CONCEPT. A systematic review of drone-based digital twins for water quality. This must be read carefully before another line of twin code is written — it is where we find out what has already been tried and what already failed.
references.verification_note: First author captured only partially. Complete the author list.

references.title: Monitoring water quality parameters of freshwater aquaculture ponds using UAV-based multispectral images
references.relevance: Directly on point: UAV multispectral for freshwater aquaculture pond water quality. The single most relevant empirical precedent for the imaging half of the OmniDrone.
references.priority: HIGHEST — most directly relevant empirical study

references.title: UAV and satellite remote sensing for inland water quality assessments: A literature review
references.relevance: Establishes the UAV-satellite complementarity that underpins our recommendation to ingest free Copernicus data for historical baselining rather than treating satellites as a competitor.

references.title: UAV-based remote sensing of chlorophyll-a concentrations in inland water bodies: A systematic review
references.relevance: The most recent systematic review of UAV chlorophyll retrieval in inland water. Directly relevant to the NDCI calibration workflow.

references.title: A meta-analysis on harmful algal bloom (HAB) detection and monitoring: A remote sensing perspective
references.relevance: Source for the finding that chlorophyll-a is the HAB proxy in roughly 80 percent of remote sensing studies. Also the source of the essential caveat this repository repeats throughout: chlorophyll indicates biomass, NOT toxicity.

references.title: A review on monitoring, forecasting, and early warning of harmful algal bloom
references.relevance: Review of HAB early warning approaches. Relevant to positioning our bloom detection against established methods.
references.verification_note: ResearchGate link only. Find the publisher version.

references.title: Using Copernicus Sentinel-2 and Sentinel-3 data to monitor harmful algal blooms in Southern Chile during the COVID-19 lockdown
references.relevance: Demonstrates OPERATIONAL satellite-based HAB early warning for aquaculture — advising decision-makers ahead of salmon mortality events. Important as evidence that this is not speculative, and as a reminder that satellites already do part of what we propose, for free, at coarser resolution.

references.title: Mapping harmful algae blooms: The potential of hyperspectral imaging technologies
references.relevance: Justifies our decision NOT to carry hyperspectral — establishes what we give up and why the multispectral trade point is defensible.

references.title: Digital twin-based intelligent fish farming with Artificial Intelligence Internet of Things (AIoT)
references.relevance: Core digital twin prior art for aquaculture. Establishes the IoT-AI fusion architecture our platform follows.

references.title: Digital twins for land-based aquaculture: A case study for rainbow trout (Oncorhynchus mykiss)
references.relevance: A demonstrated digital twin case study rather than a proposal — evidence that the twin concept has moved past hand-waving. Land-based RAS context, which is explicitly outside our market, so read for method rather than for transferable findings.

references.title: Digital twin architecture evaluation for intelligent fish farm management using modified analytic hierarchy process
references.relevance: Architecture evaluation methodology for aquaculture digital twins.

references.title: Digital twin technology for aquaculture systems simulation
references.relevance: The simulation layer that would make PondTwin an actual twin rather than a 3D dashboard.

references.title: Machine learning, deep learning and hybrid models in aquaculture: water quality, disease detection, feed optimisation and fish behaviour
references.relevance: Source for the framing of ML applications in aquaculture and for the identified challenges: data quality dependence, deployment cost, power constraints, and the emerging role of federated learning, explainable AI, edge computing, and transfer learning.
references.verification_note: This entry is a SYNTHESIS of search-result summaries and does not correspond to a single confirmed publication. It MUST be replaced with the specific review it paraphrases, or removed. As written it is not a citable source and using it would be a genuine academic integrity problem.

references.title: Catch & Culture Review: Precision aquaculture can shape greater advancements in aquaculture
references.relevance: Industry framing of precision aquaculture.
references.source_quality_note: Industry trade publication. Fine for a pitch, weak for a thesis.

references.title: How to improve your feed conversion ratio — a guide
references.relevance: Source for the widely-cited figure that feed is approximately 60 percent of aquaculture operating cost (attributed to NOAA), and for typical FCR ranges of 1.0-2.5.
references.source_quality_note: VENDOR CONTENT. It attributes the 60 percent figure to NOAA — GO TO NOAA DIRECTLY and cite the primary source. Also verify whether that US-derived figure transfers to Philippine tilapia and milkfish, which is assumed throughout this repository and is NOT established.
references.priority: HIGH — this figure is load-bearing in the value proposition and it is currently sourced to a vendor blog

references.title: A low feed conversion ratio is the primary indicator of efficient aquaculture
references.relevance: Source for the anchor calculation that at USD 500/t feed, a 0.1 FCR difference is worth USD 50 per tonne of production. This is the one externally-sourced economic figure the ROI argument rests on.

references.title: The power of feed conversion ratios in aquaculture: Driving profitability, performance, and sustainability
references.source_quality_note: Industry association with a commercial interest in feed. Note the conflict when citing.

authors: Civil Aviation Authority of the Philippines
references.title: RPAS Regulations
references.relevance: THE governing regulatory source for the entire operating concept. Source for the 7 kg gross weight bracket, registration and pilot certification requirements, VLOS as default, and BVLOS requiring specific authorisation.
references.verification_note: CRITICAL: every regulatory statement in this repository derives from SECONDARY SUMMARIES of CAAP rules found via search, NOT from the primary regulatory text. The 7 kg threshold currently drives the airframe mass target. CONFIRM DIRECTLY WITH CAAP BEFORE ANY DESIGN OR BUSINESS DECISION DEPENDS ON IT. This repository is not legal advice and its regulatory reading may be wrong.
references.priority: HIGHEST — most consequential unverified claim in the repository

authors: Civil Aviation Authority of the Philippines
references.title: Types of Operations
IEC 60529: Degrees of protection provided by enclosures (IP Code)
QARTOD — Quality Assurance/Quality Control of Real-Time Oceanographic Data manuals
references.relevance: The mature prior art for real-time water data QC. Our QC flag convention and test battery are adapted from it. Adopting an established convention rather than inventing one is both cheaper and more defensible.

references.title: Technology Readiness Level definitions
references.relevance: The TRL scale used throughout this repository to prevent the pitch implying more maturity than exists.

references.title: Model cards for model reporting
references.container: Proceedings of the Conference on Fairness, Accountability, and Transparency (FAT* '19)
references.relevance: The convention followed in ai-ml-models.json, including the mandatory out_of_scope_use field.

references.title: Aqueous ammonia equilibrium calculations: Effect of pH and temperature
references.container: Journal of the Fisheries Research Board of Canada
references.relevance: The standard reference for the un-ionised ammonia fraction calculation used in data-dictionary.json. The pKa formula in that file traces here.
references.verification_note: Reconstructed from general knowledge. Confirm before citing — the formula in the data dictionary depends on it.

references.title: On the concept of lake stability
references.relevance: The Schmidt stability formulation used for the stratification index — our flagship derived variable.

references.title: Metabolomic analysis of the Takifugu obscurus gill under acute hypoxic stress
references.relevance: Physiological evidence for hypoxic stress mechanisms. Peripheral to the core argument.

references.title: How competitive forces shape strategy
Value proposition design: How to create products and services customers want
Business model generation: A handbook for visionaries, game changers, and challengers
Environmental scanning: The impact of the stakeholder concept
references.container: Proceedings of the Second International Conference on Information Systems
references.relevance: The power-interest grid used in stakeholder-pain-points.json.

references.title: Crossing the chasm: Marketing and selling high-tech products to mainstream customers
Diffusion of innovations
references.relevance: The adoption framework underlying the socioeconomic-adoption studies file and the go-to-market reasoning.

authors: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers
references.title: IEEE 1362-1998: Guide for Information Technology — System Definition — Concept of Operations (ConOps) Document

authors: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations
references.title: Aquaculture development: Ecosystem approach to aquaculture (FAO Technical Guidelines for Responsible Fisheries No. 5, Suppl. 4)
Keep a Changelog 1.1.0

bibliography_health.the_honest_assessment: ZERO sources in this bibliography have been opened and verified. This is a research SCAFFOLD, not a finished reference list. It is genuinely useful — it maps the literature, identifies the key papers, and explains why each matters — but it is not yet citable.

priority_verification_queue.why: Drives the airframe mass target and the entire business model's BVLOS assumption. Most consequential unverified claim in the repository.
The scientific foundation of the vertical profiling argument, and it currently has no author or year.
Closest prior art to the whole concept. Read before building.
Not a real citation as written. Fix or remove.
The 60-percent-feed-cost figure is load-bearing in the value proposition and is currently sourced to a vendor blog citing NOAA. Go to NOAA.
The general water quality authority. Details unconfirmed.

known_gaps: BFAR Philippine Fisheries Profile — the national statistical yearbook. THE most important missing source in this repository. Every Philippine market number is a guess without it.
DENR water quality criteria — the primary administrative orders. water-quality-standards.json is a placeholder without them.

Species-specific DO and ammonia tolerance from primary literature for tilapia and milkfish. Currently the alert thresholds rest on general practice rather than sourced species data, which is the highest-consequence placeholder in the whole repository.
Philippine farm cost structure data. The 60-percent feed figure is US-derived and its transferability is assumed, not established.
Peer-reviewed sources on Taal Lake limnology and volcanic sulfur chemistry, which we claim as a local moat.
Prior art on UAV-tethered sonde deployment specifically — the pendulum control problem. Several academic groups have hit this wall and we should read them before rediscovering it expensively.
